compiled_state.title: [ticket_graph/_links.py]: Python push lacks retry-on-non-fast-forward that bash _push_tickets_branch implements
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Deferred Review Nitpick

#### Expected Behavior

_write_link_event's best-effort push should retry with fetch+rebase on non-fast-forward rejections, matching the 3-retry loop in bash _push_tickets_branch.

#### Actual Behavior

The Python push (added in fix 9b17-369f) is a single best-effort call with no retry logic. Under concurrent link operations two agents may produce non-fast-forward push failures silently. Given best-effort semantics this is low-risk but creates a silent parity gap with the bash implementation.
